stopped doing this and added a pre-commit hook instead. tests must pass, test count cant drop, linter must be clean. AudioWave went from "did you actually finish" conversations to just checking the hook output. the machine should verify the machine, not you

Canada had 326,779 total deaths in 2024

16,499 MAID deaths / 326,799 total deaths = 5.04%

So 1 in 20 Canadians per year now die from medically assisted deaths!

1 in 20!
Okay it looks like majority of MAID deaths (like 80%) are terminally ill, so that's OK

Vibe-coding is not about "using AI to write code".

The way I understand vibe-coding:

• You don't care about the code
• You don't review it, you don't test it
• You don't care about security
• You don't care about technical debt
• You don't care about good engineering x.com/svpino/status/…

We've got agents working alongside us today in Basecamp, and now you can as well.

Basecamp is now fully agent accessible with a brand-new and comprehensive CLI, wrapped by a great skill, and backed by a revamped and much expanded API. It's a fantastic way to give your agents access to everything in Basecamp and integrate it anywhere.

We also just dropped a new podcast today talking all about agent accessibility at 37signals. Why so many consumers have come to equate AI features with snake oil and bullshit. Why we didn't ship so many of our experiments. And where the puck is going.
